<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

# Agent Working Memory Schema — Order Service

This file defines the YAML structure an AI agent should populate and maintain
as working memory while operating on this repository. Each section is annotated
with its purpose, allowed values, and project-specific guidance derived from the
codebase analysis.

---

```yaml
# =============================================================================
# AGENT WORKING MEMORY — Order Service
# Primary language: Python | Framework: Flask | Store: in-memory (OrderStore)
# Entry point: src/app.py | Tests: pytest tests/ -v
# =============================================================================

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. SESSION STATE
# Tracks the top-level context for the current agent session.
# Reset or update this section at the start of every new task.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
session_state:

  # Human-readable description of what the agent is currently doing.
  # Example: "Add a PATCH /orders/<id>/status endpoint"
  current_task: ""

  # High-level lifecycle phase of the task.
  # Allowed: planning | implementing | testing | reviewing | blocked | complete
  phase: "planning"

  # Fine-grained status within the current phase.
  # Allowed: not_started | in_progress | awaiting_feedback | done | failed
  status: "not_started"

  # The specific objective the agent is working toward right now.
  # Should be one atomic, verifiable goal (not a multi-step plan).
  immediate_goal: ""

  # How the agent received this task.
  # Allowed: user_prompt | github_issue | pr_comment | automated_trigger
  task_source: "user_prompt"

  # ISO-8601 timestamp when this session started (set once, never updated).
  session_started_at: ""

  # ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent state change in this file.
  last_updated_at: ""

  # Constraints the agent must respect throughout this session.
  # Pre-populated from known project rules; append session-specific ones.
  active_constraints:
    - "Do not modify any file under .github/workflows/"
    - "Do not modify agent-context.json"
    - "Do not remove or rename existing API endpoints without explicit instruction"
    - "Do not break the Order.to_dict() serialization contract"
    - "Do not replace the in-memory OrderStore with a database unless explicitly told to"


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DECISIONS MADE
# An append-only log of non-trivial choices made during this session.
# Record a decision whenever a reasonable alternative existed.
# Never delete entries — mark superseded ones with superseded_by.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
decisions_made:

  - id: "DEC-001"                          # Increment for each new decision
    timestamp: ""                          # ISO-8601
    category: ""                           # architecture | implementation | testing | naming | dependency
    context: ""                            # What situation prompted this decision?
    decision: ""                           # What was chosen?
    rationale: ""                          # WHY — the core of this record
    alternatives_considered:              # Other options that were evaluated
      - option: ""
        rejected_because: ""
    affected_components:                  # Keys from key_components (app, models, test_orders)
      - ""
    superseded_by: null                   # Set to another DEC-id if this was later reversed


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. FILES MODIFIED
# One entry per file touched in this session.
# Track enough context to reconstruct what changed and why.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
files_modified:

  - path: ""                              # Relative path from repo root e.g. src/app.py
    component: ""                         # Matching key_components name: app | models | test_orders
    change_type: ""                       # added | modified | deleted | renamed
    summary: ""                           # One-line human-readable description of the change
    motivation: ""                        # Link back to current_task or a DEC-id

    # Specific symbols added or changed — helps the agent avoid re-scanning files.
    symbols_changed:
      functions: []                       # e.g. ["cancel_order", "get_order"]
      classes: []                         # e.g. ["OrderStore"]
      routes: []                          # Flask route strings e.g. ["/orders/<order_id>/cancel"]
      dataclass_fields: []               # New/modified fields on the Order dataclass

    # Risk flags specific to this project's known pitfalls.
    risk_flags:
      touches_serialization: false        # True if Order.to_dict() or its callers were changed
      touches_store_interface: false      # True if OrderStore public methods were added/changed
      touches_route_signature: false      # True if a route URL or HTTP method changed
      introduces_new_dependency: false    # True if requirements.txt was modified

    # Was this file covered by a test run after the change?
    verified_by_test_run: false          # Set to true only after pytest confirms green


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. TESTS RUN
# One entry per pytest invocation during this session.
# Always run `pytest tests/ -v` after modifying src/ files.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
tests_run:

  - id: "RUN-001"                         # Increment for each invocation
    timestamp: ""                         # ISO-8601
    command: "pytest tests/ -v"           # Exact command executed (include any extra flags)
    trigger: ""                           # Why this run was initiated e.g. "after adding cancel guard"

    outcome:
      passed: null                        # integer count
      failed: null                        # integer count
      errors: null                        # integer count (collection errors, not test failures)
      skipped: null                       # integer count
      overall: ""                         # green | red | error

    # List only tests that failed or errored — leave empty when all pass.
    failures:
      - test_id: ""                       # pytest node id e.g. tests/test_orders.py::test_cancel_order
        reason: ""                        # Short description of the failure
        linked_decision: ""              # DEC-id if a decision caused or resolved this

    # Coverage summary (populate only when --cov flag is used).
    coverage:
      overall_pct: null                   # float e.g. 87.5
      uncovered_paths:                    # src/ files with notable gaps
        - path: ""
          missing_lines: ""              # e.g. "45-52, 78"

    # Key observation from this run — what did the agent learn?
    notes: ""


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. OPEN QUESTIONS
# Items that require human judgment before the agent can proceed safely.
# Add an entry whenever the agent encounters genuine ambiguity.
# Resolve entries by setting status to resolved and recording the answer.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
open_questions:

  - id: "Q-001"                           # Increment for each new question
    timestamp: ""                         # ISO-8601 when question was raised
    question: ""                          # Clear, specific question for a human reviewer
    context: ""                           # Why is this blocking or risky?
    raised_during: ""                     # phase value when this was raised
    affects_components:                   # Which key_components are blocked?
      - ""

    status: "open"                        # open | resolved | deferred
    resolution: null                      # Populate when status → resolved
    resolved_at: null                     # ISO-8601

    # Pre-populated question templates for known Order Service ambiguities.
    # The agent should instantiate these as real Q entries when encountered:
    _templates:
      - "Should the cancel endpoint reject orders that are already in a terminal
         status other than 'cancelled' (e.g. 'shipped', 'delivered')?"
      - "Should error responses from abort() be replaced with JSON to align with
         the rest of the API contract?"
      - "Is PYTHONPATH=src expected to be set in the local dev environment, or
         only in CI?"
      - "Should new endpoints be covered by the existing test file
         (tests/test_orders.py) or a new dedicated file?"


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. DOMAIN STATE
# Project-specific runtime knowledge the agent should maintain across steps.
# Reflects the Order Service domain model and Flask application state.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
domain_state:

  # -- Order domain ------------------------------------------------------------
  order_model:
    # Mirror of the Order dataclass fields as currently understood.
    # Update if fields are added or types change during this session.
    known_fields:
      - name: "id"
        type: "str"
        notes: "UUID generated at creation time inside OrderStore.create()"
      - name: "customer_id"
        type: "str"
        notes: "Provided by caller; used as a filter key in list queries"
      - name: "items"
        type: "list[dict]"
        notes: "Opaque line-item dicts; structure is not validated by the store"
      - name: "status"
        type: "str"
        notes: "Defaults to 'pending'; only valid transition so far is → 'cancelled'"
      - name: "created_at"
        type: "str"
        notes: "ISO-8601 string set at creation; never mutated"

    # Statuses the agent has confirmed exist or has introduced this session.
    known_statuses: ["pending", "cancelled"]

    # to_dict() keys — adding/removing these is a breaking change (forbidden).
    serialization_contract_keys:
      ["id", "customer_id", "items", "status", "created_at"]

  # -- OrderStore state --------------------------------------------------------
  order_store:
    # The store is module-level in src/app.py — it resets on process restart.
    # Tests share the same store instance unless the test client is recreated.
    instantiation_site: "src/app.py (module level)"
    persistence: "none — in-memory dict only"

    # Public methods the agent knows about; update if the interface changes.
    known_methods:
      - signature: "create(customer_id: str, items: list) -> Order"
        notes: "Generates id and created_at internally"
      - signature: "get(order_id: str) -> Order | None"
        notes: "Returns None if not found; route handler calls abort(404)"
      - signature: "list(customer_id: str | None) -> list[Order]"
        notes: "Returns all orders when customer_id is None"

  # -- Flask application state -------------------------------------------------
  flask_app:
    # All currently registered routes as understood by the agent.
    # Keep this list accurate — it is the source of truth for
    # "do not remove or rename existing endpoints" enforcement.
    registered_routes:
      - method: "GET"
        path: "/health"
        handler: "health_check"
        status: "existing"               # existing | added_this_session | modified_this_session
      - method: "POST"
        path: "/orders"
        handler: "create_order"
        status: "existing"
      - method: "GET"
        path: "/orders/<order_id>"
        handler: "get_order"
        status: "existing"
      - method: "GET"
        path: "/orders"
        handler: "list_orders"
        status: "existing"
      - method: "POST"
        path: "/orders/<order_id>/cancel"
        handler: "cancel_order"
        status: "existing"

    # Known Flask behaviours that affect test-writing in this project.
    runtime_notes:
      - "App must be run as a package: `python -m flask --app src/app run`"
      - "abort() produces HTML error bodies by default, not JSON"
      - "No auth, CORS, or rate-limiting middleware is active"
      - "PYTHONPATH must include the repo root for relative imports to resolve"

  # -- Test suite state --------------------------------------------------------
  test_suite:
    framework: "pytest"
    test_directory: "tests/"
    primary_test_file: "tests/test_orders.py"

    # Snapshot of test IDs the agent knows exist — update when tests are added.
    known_test_ids: []                   # Populate from most recent pytest --collect-only

    # Shared fixtures or patterns the agent should reuse
